race.comment: ACHESON (3) tackled open company at Canberra last start and did a reasonable job to be beaten three lengths over 1200m. Strips fitter now and the extra 100m is in his favour. Draws a nice gate so there are a few positives to steer his way in a tricky race. GLOWING RAPIDS (6) led and gave a kick but was mowed down late and finished third in a BM58 over this distance at Dubbo last time out. Consistent gelding who gets a little weight relief today. Races well here so his credentials are sound. KEN'KER (8) dropped in grade to a BM50 at Tuncurry recently and absolutely bolted in, leading all the way in the 1400m contest. Can run another cheeky race on a nine-day back-up. THE ARTEFACT (10) was easily accounted for on a bog track at Orange last start. Down in class this time and a firmer surface will help. A BOY NAMED SOO (7) has defied the betting to win two from two this prep. Hard to knock in that sort of form.
